isolation.testingApproach: pytest + code inspection (Flet desktop app, no browser available)

assertions.title: Profile creation via onboarding
assertions.description: New users can create profile via onboarding screen with name, goals, and experience level

steps.observed: OnboardingScreen.__init__ creates all three form fields with appropriate labels and widths (400px). Experience dropdown has beginner/intermediate/advanced options.

steps.expected: Validates name, calls api_client.create_profile, navigates to home on success
steps.observed: handle_continue validates name is not empty, calls api_client.create_profile(name, goals, experience), navigates to /home on success. Shows error message on failure.
All 16 home_page tests pass, including profile display validation tests

codeSnippets: onboarding_screen.py:handle_continue validates name with 'if not name: self.show_error("Please enter your name")'
onboarding_screen.py:api_client.create_profile(name, goals, experience) called on valid submission

assertions.title: Profile editing via profile_page
assertions.description: Users can edit their profile via profile_page with pre-filled form

steps.expected: ProfilePage loads existing profile and pre-fills form fields
steps.observed: load_profile calls api_client.get_profile(), populates name_field.value, goals_field.value, experience_dropdown.value with existing data. Edit button on home page navigates to /profile.

steps.expected: Validates name, calls api_client.update_profile, shows success and returns home
steps.observed: handle_save validates name is not empty, calls api_client.update_profile(name, goals, experience), shows success message 'Profile saved successfully!', navigates to /home after 1.5s delay.

steps.expected: Form shows existing values when editing
steps.observed: profile_page.py:load_profile sets self.name_field.value = result.get('name', ''), self.goals_field.value = result.get('goals', '') or '', self.experience_dropdown.value = experience

codeSnippets: profile_page.py:load_profile pre-fills form: name_field.value, goals_field.value, experience_dropdown.value
profile_page.py:handle_save calls api_client.update_profile and shows success message

assertions.title: Name and goals prominently displayed on home
assertions.description: Home page displays user's name and meditation goals prominently

steps.expected: Welcome header with name (large, bold) and profile card showing goals
steps.observed: Welcome container shows 'Welcome, {self.user_name}' with size=32 and weight=BOLD. Profile card shows Name, Goals, Experience in labeled rows.

steps.expected: Tests confirm name is prominent and goals are displayed
steps.observed: test_profile_display_val_profile_003_name_prominent passes - verifies name is in welcome_text with size=32 and weight=BOLD. test_profile_display_val_profile_003_goals_displayed passes - verifies goals text appears in profile card.

steps.expected: load_profile updates user_name, meditation_goals from API response
steps.observed: home_page.py:load_profile sets self.user_name = result.get('name', 'Guest User'), self.meditation_goals = result.get('goals', 'Not set') or 'Not set'

evidence.testOutput: 16 tests passed in test_home_page.py including TestHomePageValidation tests
codeSnippets: home_page.py:Profile card shows 'Name:', 'Goals:', 'Experience:' in labeled rows

assertions.title: Settings persistence via backend API
assertions.description: Settings (TTS voice, STT enabled, AI model) persist via backend API

steps.observed: handle_tts_voice_change, handle_stt_enabled_change, handle_model_change all call save_settings() which calls api_client.update_settings(updates)

steps.expected: Sends settings to API, shows success/error status
steps.observed: save_settings calls api_client.update_settings(updates), shows status message 'Settings saved' or 'Failed to save: {error}'

steps.expected: Settings loaded from API on page load
steps.observed: load_settings calls api_client.get_settings(), populates tts_voice.value, stt_enabled.value, ai_model.value from loaded settings

codeSnippets: settings_page.py:save_settings calls api_client.update_settings with dict of updated values
settings_page.py:load_settings populates UI from api_client.get_settings result

assertions.title: RAM display in settings
assertions.description: Settings page displays system RAM using platform-specific detection

steps.expected: get_local_ram_gb detects RAM and displays it in System RAM section
steps.observed: get_local_ram_gb() uses subprocess.run(['sysctl', '-n', 'hw.memsize']) on Darwin, /proc/meminfo on Linux, kernel32 on Windows. Result displayed as 'X.X GB' in System RAM section.

steps.expected: ram_display Text control shows detected RAM
steps.observed: self.ram_display is a ft.Text control initialized as 'Detecting...'. After load_system_ram or load_settings, it shows '{ram_gb:.1f} GB'. Falls back to 'Unknown' if detection fails.

steps.action: Review router for system RAM endpoint
steps.expected: Backend provides GET /api/settings/system with RAM info
steps.observed: load_settings calls api_client.get_system_info() as fallback. Router endpoint get_system_ram_gb in routers/settings.py implements platform-specific detection.

codeSnippets: settings_page.py:get_local_ram_gb() uses platform.system()=='Darwin' check with sysctl command
settings_page.py:ram_display shows f'{ram_gb:.1f} GB' after detection

assertions.title: NavigationRail responsive layout
assertions.description: Navigation sidebar uses NavigationRail with responsive min_width

steps.expected: NavigationRail with destinations and responsive width settings
steps.observed: NavigationRail has min_width=100, min_extended_width=200, label_type=ALL. Destinations include Home, History, Settings, Profile with appropriate icons.

steps.action: Verify leading avatar and trailing logout
steps.expected: App logo in leading position, logout button in trailing position
steps.observed: leading=ft.Container with CircleAvatar showing 'M' in deep purple. trailing=ft.Container with expand=True containing IconButton with logout icon at bottom.

steps.expected: NavigationRail used in Row with VerticalDivider and content Container
steps.observed: home_page.py, profile_page.py, settings_page.py all use ft.Row([navigation.build(), ft.VerticalDivider(width=1), ft.Container(expand=True, content=...)])

summary: Tested 6 assertions (VAL-PROFILE-001, VAL-PROFILE-002, VAL-PROFILE-003, VAL-SETTINGS-001, VAL-SETTINGS-005, VAL-UI-005): All PASS. Full test suite: 94 passed. Note: This is a Flet desktop app, tested via pytest and code inspection since no browser/display available for visual UI testing.
